summary: Comprehensive UI testing of 7 remaining bugs. MAJOR-01 (target suggestion showing 'sex' instead of 'charges') CONFIRMED. MINOR-11 (deploy confirmation dialog not appearing) CONFIRMED. Other bugs could not be fully tested due to training timeouts and session state issues.

ui_bugs.issue: MAJOR-01: When loading test CSV with 'charges' column, the Dataset Summary shows 'Suggested target: sex' instead of 'charges'. The 'charges' column (financial keyword) should be prioritized for regression but 'sex' (categorical with 2 classes) is being suggested as primary target.
ui_bugs.selector: Dataset Summary section, green suggestion box
ui_bugs.evidence: Screenshot shows 'Suggested target: sex - Detected as categorical with 2 classes - suitable for classification' while 'charges' suggestion appears separately in Model Configuration section

ui_bugs.issue: MINOR-11: Deploy confirmation dialog (data-testid='deploy-confirm-dialog') does NOT appear when clicking Deploy button. The model appears to deploy directly without showing the confirmation dialog with Cancel/Confirm buttons.
ui_bugs.evidence: Clicked Deploy button, waited 1.5 seconds, dialog element not found in DOM

action_items: MAJOR-01: Fix target suggestion logic in App.js to prioritize 'charges' (financial keyword) over 'sex' for insurance dataset. The financialKeywords scoring (+25 for numeric) should outweigh categorical scoring (+10 + uniqueCount bonus)

MINOR-11: Debug why setConfirmDeploy(model) at DeployView.js line 122 is not triggering the AnimatePresence dialog at lines 227-274. Check if confirmDeploy state is being set correctly and if the dialog is rendering
critical_code_review_comments: MAJOR-01: The suggestedTarget logic at App.js:773-810 gives +25 to financial keywords for numeric columns and +10 to categorical with 2-10 classes. For 'sex' (2 classes), score = 10 + (10-2) = 18. For 'charges' (numeric, financial keyword), score should be 25 + variability bonus. The bug may be in how the 'best' suggestion is displayed in Dataset Summary vs Model Configuration.

MINOR-11: The Deploy button onClick={() => setConfirmDeploy(model) should trigger the dialog. The AnimatePresence wrapper at line 227 checks {confirmDeploy && ...}. Possible issues: (1) model object is null/undefined, (2) React state not updating, (3) AnimatePresence not rendering

success_rate.frontend: 2/7 bugs verified (MAJOR-01, MINOR-11 confirmed as issues)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Training with Auto algorithm takes >20 seconds. Use Insurance Costs sample dataset for faster testing. The test CSV at /datasets/test_insurance.csv has 'east' as rare region (1 occurrence out of 100 rows). For MINOR-05/09 testing, need to complete training first then check region dropdown options. For MINOR-07, need a model with negative R2 (Decision Tree on small noisy data may produce this). MINOR-12 requires: load dataset -> Preprocess tab -> Apply All -> Data Explorer -> check for blue banner.

MAJOR-01.description: Target suggestion shows 'sex' instead of 'charges' for insurance dataset
MAJOR-01.evidence: Dataset Summary shows 'Suggested target: sex' while 'charges' appears in separate suggestion box
MAJOR-01.root_cause: suggestedTarget logic in App.js may be prioritizing categorical columns over financial keywords

MAJOR-03.reason: Training did not complete in time to test predictions

MINOR-05.reason: No trained models available to test Predictions tab

MINOR-07.description: Negative R2 warning in Compare tab
MINOR-07.reason: No models with negative R2 available

MINOR-09.reason: No trained models available to test What-If tab

MINOR-11.evidence: Clicked Deploy button, dialog element [data-testid='deploy-confirm-dialog'] not found
MINOR-11.root_cause: setConfirmDeploy(model) may not be triggering state update or AnimatePresence not rendering

MINOR-12.reason: No dataset loaded when navigating to Data Explorer

testing_limitations: Training with Auto algorithm takes >20 seconds, causing timeouts
Session state resets between browser automation runs
Need to complete full workflow (load data -> train -> navigate) in single session
